Patch Set 2:

James, I don't see any comments. Could you publish them please?

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

Hi Dmitry,

In run_one(), tfile and tdir are defined as follows:

        export tfile=f${testnum}.${TESTSUITE}
        export tdir=d${testnum}.${TESTSUITE}

They are unique to each sub-test, and cleaned up at the end of sanity test suite in check_and_cleanup_lustre():

        [ -n "$DIR" ] && rm -rf $DIR/[Rdfs][0-9]* ||
                error "remove sub-test dirs failed"

So, we do not need cleanup them in each sub-test.

Patch Set 2:

(5 comments)

My comments were under patch 1. I've copied the to patch 2.

Again, if you modify the patch, here are a few things that could be included.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

I don't think that the files should be deleted after every test. That adds clutter to the debug logs, and makes the tests more complex for no benefit. 

I agree that there shouldn't be generic "rm -r" lines, and that should be fixed.

The only subtests that should clean up are those that create hundreds of files or many MB of data.
Uploaded patch set 3.

parents.subject: LU-3953 build: fix paths for generated headers

commit.subject: LU-5047 tests: correct cleanup files in sanity.sh
commit.message:
LU-5047 tests: correct cleanup files in sanity.sh

Don't remove all files from Lustre share. Create files that will be
cleaned up at the end tests session in check_and_cleanup_lustre().

Fix cleanup for test_54c.

Signed-off-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
Change-Id: I23624b9d14e7de3423b82b4a5b8508c4820c55d4
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/10291
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: James Nunez <james.a.nunez@intel.com>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
